info.title: Default module
info.description:
Hello and thank you for automating with Dolphin{anty}!

## Support

For any questions, please contact our support (you can find support in the Dolphin{anty} [website](https://dolphin-anty.com) or in Dolphin{anty} app – just click at the right bottom corner).

## LLM-friendly documentation

If you are looking for docs which you can paste into your LLM, just press _"Download OpenAPI document"_ link above. The document is in format that all LLMs understand.

## Authorization
Authorization is done via a _JWT token_, which you can obtain from the Dolphin{anty} website: [https://dolphin-anty.com/panel](https://dolphin-anty.com/panel/index.html#/api).

Fill the name and token expiration, then click 'generate token'. You will be presented with a token that you can copy and paste into the `Authorization` header.

The token is shown only once, so make sure to copy it before closing the page.


You **must** present token in the `Authorization` header for **every request**.

## Rate limiting
The API is rate limited to 500 requests per minute. If you exceed this limit, you will receive a 429 status code.

Rate limits are subject to change and fluctuate based on the load on the API, so you may receive a 429 status code even if you are within the limit.

To be sure you are not exceeding the limit, you can use those headers:
- `x-ratelimiter-allowed-rph`
- `x-ratelimiter-allowed-rpm`
- `x-ratelimiter-allowed-rps`
- `x-ratelimiter-rph-left`
- `x-ratelimiter-rpm-left`
- `x-ratelimiter-rps-left`

They will be sent with every request.

## Error handling
The API returns standard HTTP status codes for errors. If the request is successful, the status code will be 200.
If the request is not successful, the status code will be 400 or 500.
The response body will contain a JSON object with the error message.

## Types generation

You can generate types for your language of choice by using [openapi-typescript](https://github.com/drwpow/openapi-typescript) or [openapi-generator](https://openapi-generator.tech/).

By doing so, you will be able to use the API in your code without having to manually parse the response. You will have all the types and methods available in your IDE, with autocompletion and type checking. And the types will be updated automatically in case of any changes in the API.

We're highly recommending to do so, as it will make your life much easier.

#### Python types generation example

**Basic setup:**
```bash
pip install openapi-generator-cli
curl -o openapi.yaml http://docs.dolphin-anty-cdn.com/openapi.yaml
openapi-generator generate -i openapi.yaml -g python -o ./python-client
pip install -e ./python-client
```

_Tip: Whichever language you use, run the **type generation and client setup** step in your CI pipeline or as a git hook so that every change to our types in `openapi.yaml` automatically refreshes your typed client._

**Usage in code:**
```python
from openapi_client import ApiClient, Configuration
from openapi_client.api import proxies_api
from openapi_client.models import ProxyCreateInput

configuration = Configuration()
configuration.api_key['Authorization'] = 'YOUR_API_KEY'
configuration.api_key_prefix['Authorization'] = 'Bearer'

api_instance = proxies_api.ProxiesApi(api_client=ApiClient(configuration))
proxy_create_input = ProxyCreateInput(
    type="http",
    host="proxy.example.com",
    port=8080,
    login="username",
    password="password"
)

api_instance.create_proxy(proxy_create_input)
```

#### TypeScript type-safe client examples

**First generate static types once:**
```bash
npx openapi-typescript openapi.yaml -o src/api.d.ts
```

_Tip: Whichever language you use, run the **type generation and client setup** step in your CI pipeline or as a git hook so that every change to our types in `openapi.yaml` automatically refreshes your typed client._


##### Pattern 1 – openapi-fetch (zero-codegen)

```bash
npm install openapi-fetch openapi-typescript
```

```typescript
import createClient from 'openapi-fetch';
import type { paths } from './api';

const client = createClient<paths>({
  baseUrl: 'https://dolphin-anty-api.com',
  headers: { Authorization: 'Bearer YOUR_API_KEY' },
});

const { data } = await client.POST('/proxy', { body: proxyCreateInput });
```

##### Pattern 2 – openapi-client-axios (Axios wrapper)

```bash
npm install axios openapi-client-axios openapi-typescript
```

```typescript
import OpenAPIClientAxios from 'openapi-client-axios';
import type { paths } from './api';

const api = new OpenAPIClientAxios<paths>({
  definition: 'http://docs.dolphin-anty-cdn.com/openapi.yaml',
});
const client = await api.init();

await client.createProxy(null, proxyCreateInput);
```

## Guarantees

We are trying not to break things, but sometimes we have to.

When this happens, we are notifying our users via:

1. Our Telegram channel: [@dolphin_anty](https://t.me/dolphin_anty)
2. Our app notifications (top right corner, icon with bell).

When an endpoint is undergoing for a planned deprecation, we will also mark it as deprecated here, in this Swagger/OpenAPI document.

folderColor.description: Sets the color value of the folder. Optional values: `#3370FF`, `#209E91`, `#FB9247`, `#00B8EB`.

folderName.description: Sets the folder name. Must be unique and cannot duplicate any existing name.

value.message: Folder name already exists. Please choose another name.
Folder name already exists. Please choose another name.

post.description: Batch update folders. If only one folder needs to be updated, just provide the information for that folder.

os.description:
The operation system value, options should be:

{
  WINDOWS = 'Win32',
  MAC = 'MacIntel',
  LINUX = 'Linux x86_64',
  IOS = 'iPhone',
  ANDROID = 'Linux armv81',
}

allowMultipleOpenings.description: Whether to allow multiple openings: 0 not allowed 1 allowed

urls.description: URLs to open in the browser, separated by commas

proxyId.description: Proxy id, if you want to select an existing proxy, you need to fill in the proxy id

proxyMethod.description: Proxy method, you can choose from the following: 0-base, 1-dynamic, 2-custom, 4-select existing proxy

proxy.description: You can add a custom proxy, or select an existing proxy, if you want to select an existing proxy, you need to fill in the proxyId, it is recommended to use the proxyMethod 4 to select an existing proxy, if you still want to add a custom proxy, then refer to the proxy creation interface input parameters

os.description: Operating system type, when the operating system type is PC, the selected Win32, MacIntel, Linux x86_64, iPhone, Linux armv81

coreVersion.description: Browser core, default to latest. Options: 142, 140, 138, 136, 134, 132, 130.

uamodel.description: UA parameter model part, only fill in when the os is Android

product.description: Browser core value, default to chrome

timeZoneFollowIp.description: Whether to generate the corresponding time zone based on IP

geolocationFollowIp.description: Whether to generate the corresponding geolocation based on IP

uiLanguageType.description: Display language, whether to generate the corresponding language based on IP, 1-based on IP, 2-follow system, 3-custom

languageFollowIp.description: Whether to generate the corresponding language based on IP

deviceInfoEnabled.description: Whether to open custom device name

doNotTrack.description: Do Not Track, default-open, true-open, false-close

preference.description: Profile preference, if you want to keep the default, you can omit this parameter

fingerprint.fonts: Chiller;Chiller;Chiller-Regular,Miriam Mono CLM;Miriam Mono CLM Book Oblique;MiriamMonoCLM-BookOblique,Franklin Gothic Heavy;Franklin Gothic Heavy;FranklinGothic-Heavy,Bodoni MT;Bodoni MT Bold Italic;BodoniMT-BoldItalic,Noto Sans;Noto Sans SemiBold Display;Noto-Sans-SemiBold-Display,Pristina;Pristina;Pristina-Regular,Noto Sans;Noto Sans SemiBold;Noto-Sans-SemiBold,Gill Sans MT;Gill Sans MT Bold Italic;GillSansMT-BoldItalic,Italic;Italic_IV50;Italic_IV50,Monospac821 BT;Monospace 821 BT;Monospace821BT-Roman
fingerprint.mediaDeviceNoiseValue: {"videoinput":["Integrated Camera (174f:181d)","Integrated Webcam (0bda:576d)","USB2.0 UVC HD Webcam (13d3:5188)"],"audiooutput":["ZoomAudioDevice (Virtual)","Communications - Speaker/HP (3- Realtek High Definition Audio)","GX32 (2- NVIDIA High Definition Audio)"],"audioinput":["Default - Digital Microphone (Cirrus Logic High Definition Audio)","Digital Microphone (Cirrus Logic High Definition Audio)","Default - Internal Microphone (Conexant 20672 SmartAudio HD)"]}
fingerprint.userAgent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/134.0.0.0 Safari/537.36
fingerprint.webGLRender: ANGLE (AMD, AMD Radeon Pro WX 3100 Direct3D11 vs_5_0 ps_5_0, D3D11-31.0.14051.5006)

post.description:
Allows batch updating of the folders bound to profiles.

`folderId` can be obtained from the `/api/folder/list` endpoint or returned by the `/api/folder/add` endpoint.
Allows bulk-assigning a single proxy to multiple profiles.

protocol.description: Proxy protocol, Optional values: socks5, http, https, ssh

post.description: Allows bulk-assigning a single proxy to multiple profiles.

extractMethod.description:
0: extract new IP on each profile opening.
1: extract new IP only when previous IP expires.

post.description: Modify the profile’s proxy information by selecting one from the existing proxy list.

Open a profile window using `profileID`. 

The `profileID` can be obtained from the `/api/profile/getProfiles` endpoint or returned by `/api/profile/quickCreateProfile`. 

It is recommended to use `quickCreateProfile` to create the profile and record the returned ID.

protocol.description: Optional values: socks5, https, http, ssh

post.description: Add proxy information. The added proxy can be used for assigning to a profile.

protocol.description: proxy protocol, could be: socks5, http, https, ssh

proxyMethod.description: The default value is `0`; no changes are required.

publicViewing.description:
Access permissions. Optional values:

0: Me
1: Team view
2: Team edit

rotateUrl.description: If your proxy information supports refreshing via `rotateUrl`, you can fill in this field.

protocol.description: proxy protocol, could be: socks5, http, https, ssh

publicViewing.description:
Access permissions. Optional values:

0: Me
1: Team view
2: Team edit

rotateUrl.description: If your proxy information supports refreshing via `rotateUrl`, you can fill in this field.

protocol.description: proxy protocol, could be: socks5, http, https, ssh

extractMethod.description:
0: extract new IP on each profile opening.
1: extract new IP only when previous IP expires.

publicViewing.description:
Access permissions. Optional values:

0: Me
1: Team view
2: Team edit

protocol.description: proxy protocol, could be: socks5, http, https, ssh

extractMethod.description:
0: extract new IP on each profile opening.
1: extract new IP only when previous IP expires.

Access permissions. Optional values:

0: Me
1: Team view
2: Team edit

current_page.description: Current page number of the results set.

first_page_url.description: Absolute URL pointing to the first page of results.

from.description: Index (1-based) of the first item returned in the current page, or null if the collection is empty.

last_page.description: Total number of pages available based on the current filter.

last_page_url.description: Absolute URL pointing to the last page of results.

next_page_url.description: Absolute URL for the next page of results, or null if this is the last page.

path.description: Base path for the paginated resource (without query parameters).

per_page.description: Maximum number of items returned per page.

prev_page_url.description: Absolute URL for the previous page of results, or null if this is the first page.

to.description: Index (1-based) of the last item returned in the current page, or null if the collection is empty.

total.description: Total number of items across all pages.

id.description: 从界面中环境列表，点击配置后复制，或者从云手机环境list接口中获取
id.title: 云手机环境ID

name.description: Human-readable alias for the proxy. Optional. Used in the UI to fastly recognize or search for the proxy. If skipped, the proxy will be named after it is host, port, login (if provided) and password (if provided), i.e. `protocol://login:password@host:port`.

changeIpUrl.description: URL that triggers IP rotation for the proxy. Optional. Some mobile proxy provider give the ability to rotate IP by sending a request to a special URL, and our app gives user a button to do that.

provider.description: Provider identifier if the proxy originates from a supported provider. Optional.

ip.description: Public IP address detected for this proxy (same value as `lastCheck.ip` in legacy payloads). Returned by the backend.

savedByUser.description: Indicates whether the proxy was explicitly created by the user (`true`) or auto-imported (`false`). Returned by the backend.

browser_profiles_count.description: Number of browser profiles linked to this proxy (that are using this proxy).

oneOf.description: Result of the last connectivity check. The check is performed by the app on your PCs.

createdAt.description: Date and time when the last connectivity check was performed.

status.description: Indicates whether the proxy passed the connectivity check (`true`) or failed (`false`).

ip.description: Public IP address detected during the connectivity check.

country.description: Country associated with the detected IP address.

region.description: Region (state or province) associated with the detected IP address.

city.description: City associated with the detected IP address.

timezone.description: Time zone of the detected IP address location.

found.description: Number of blacklist services that reported the proxy's IP.

total.description: Total number of blacklist services that were checked.

blacklists.description: Names of blacklist services where the proxy's IP was found.

proxy_create.description: Parameters required to create a proxy. Most of those parameters will be given for you by your proxy provider. The connection to proxy will be established by the app on your PCs.

name.description: Human-readable alias for the proxy. Optional. Used in the UI to fastly recognize or search for the proxy. If skipped, the proxy will be named after it is host, port, login (if provided) and password (if provided), i.e. `protocol://login:password@host:port`.

changeIpUrl.description: URL that triggers IP rotation for the proxy. Optional. Some mobile proxy provider give the ability to rotate IP by sending a request to a special URL, and our app gives user a button to do that.

provider.description: Provider identifier if the proxy originates from a supported provider. Optional.

success.description: Indicates whether the operation was successful.

allOf.description: Fields identical to creation but intended for updates.

browser_profile_status.description: A custom status label that can be assigned to Browser profiles to quickly group or filter them in the UI.

teamId.description: Team identifier that owns the status.

userId.description: User identifier that created the status.

name.description: Human-readable name of the status. Shown in the UI near the Browser profile name.

color.description: Color used to render the status badge in the UI.

deleted.description: 1 if the status was deleted and is kept only for historical reasons, 0 otherwise.

browser_profile_status_create.description: Parameters required to create a Browser profile status.

name.description: Human-readable name of the status.

color.description: Color used to render the status badge in the UI.

type.description: Status type. Currently only **common** is supported and will be used by default if omitted. Reserved for future extensions.

profile.description: Identifier of a Browser profile to immediately assign this newly created status to. Optional – if omitted the status will not be applied to any profile.

password.description: Password of the Browser profile given in **profile** when that profile is protected by a password. Required only if **profile** is provided and the profile has a password protection enabled.

browser_profile_status_bulk_delete.description: Delete multiple Browser profile statuses at once.

ids.description: Identifiers of Browser profile statuses to delete.

allOf.description: Fields identical to creation but intended for updates.

browser_profile_status_bulk_change.description: Change the status for multiple Browser profiles in a single request.

ids.description: Identifiers of Browser profiles whose status should be changed.

status.description: Defines the desired status – either by referencing an existing status **id** or by providing **name** and **color** to create a new one on-the-fly.

id.description: Identifier of an existing Browser profile status. Optional if you want to create a brand-new status instead.

name.description: New status name to create (used when **id** is omitted).

color.description: Color of the new status (used when **id** is omitted).

Duration.description:
A Duration represents the elapsed time between two instants
as an int64 nanosecond count. The representation limits the
largest representable duration to approximately 290 years.

extension.description: Extension that can be attached to Browser profiles. Extensions can either originate from the Chrome Web Store ("link" type) or be uploaded as a local `.zip` archive ("file" type).

name.description: Display name of the extension. When the extension is added from the Chrome Web Store it is parsed automatically from the store page. When the extension is uploaded from ZIP the name is taken from `extensionName` (or the uploaded file name if omitted).

extensionId.description: 32-character identifier of the Chrome Web Store extension. Empty for ZIP-uploaded extensions.

url.description: Link that Dolphin{anty} will use to download the CRX/ZIP. Either the Chrome Web Store URL of the extension or the public S3 URL of the uploaded ZIP.

favicon.description: Data URI or URL of extension icon (up to 256 KB). Returned only when available.

sharedToEntireTeam.description: If `true`, the extension is visible and can be used by every member of the team.

mainWebsite.description: Websites where the extension is primarily used. Can help the client show contextual suggestions in the UI.

extension_create.description: Parameters required to add an extension from the Chrome Web Store. Only **public** extensions from the store can be added – private/unpublished ones will be rejected.

url.description:
Full URL of the extension on the Chrome Web Store, e.g. `https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/adblock/gighmmpiobklfepjocnamgkkbiglidom`. The URL **must** start with one of the following prefixes:
* `https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/`
* `https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/`

sharedToEntireTeam.description: If `true`, the extension will become visible to every member of your team. Otherwise only you will be able to attach it to Browser profiles.

mainWebsite.description: List of websites where the extension is mainly used. This data helps the UI to display contextual suggestions.

extension_bulk_delete.description: Identifiers of the extensions to delete.

ids.description: List of extension identifiers to delete.

extension_upload.description: Multipart form fields required to upload a ZIP-packed extension. Once the file is uploaded it will be stored on Dolphin{anty} S3 and a public URL will be generated automatically.

file.description: ZIP archive containing unpacked Chrome extension (exactly what you see when you download an extension as `.crx` and then unzip it). Maximum size – **50 MB**.

icon.description: Optional custom icon that will be shown in the UI instead of taking the icon from the ZIP.

extensionName.description: Optional custom display name. If omitted, the filename of the uploaded ZIP will be used.

sharedToEntireTeam.description: Same meaning as in the link-based upload.

mainWebsite.description: Websites where the extension is mainly used.

folder.description: A folder used to organise Browser profiles into groups. One folder can contain many Browser profiles and is shared within the team.

createdBy.description: Identifier of the user who created the folder.

teamId.description: Identifier of the team that owns the folder.

name.description: Human-readable name of the folder shown in the UI.

emoji.description: Optional emoji icon rendered near the name to improve visual scanning.

order.description: Position of the folder in the user-defined sort order (higher value means higher in the list).

isPinned.description: Indicates whether the folder is pinned by the current user.

browserProfilesData.description: Quick summary information about Browser profiles contained in the folder.

access.description: Access flags granted to the current user for the profile.

recoveryCount.description: Amount of recovery attempts triggered for the profile if any.

folder_create.description: Parameters required to create a folder.

name.description: Human-readable name of the folder.

emoji.description: Optional emoji icon rendered near the name to improve visual scanning.

isPinned.description: Whether to pin the newly created folder for the current user.

allOf.description: Fields identical to creation but intended for updates.

folder_order_update.description: Re-ordering payload allowing to drag-and-drop folders in the UI.

folders.description: List of folders with their new order index (higher value ‑ higher in list).

order.description: New order index for the folder.

folder_attach_profiles.description: Attach existing Browser profiles to a folder.

profileIds.description: Identifiers of Browser profiles to attach.

password.description: Optional password that will be applied to each attached profile if it was protected.

folder_detach_profiles.description: Remove Browser profiles from a folder.

profileIds.description: Identifiers of Browser profiles to detach from their folders.

folder_profile_ids.description: List of Browser profile identifiers contained in a folder and whether at least one of them is password-protected.

profileIds.description: Browser profile identifiers assigned to this folder.

hasProfileWithPassword.description: Indicates whether any of the profiles in the folder is protected with a password.

proxy_list_item.description: A lightweight proxy object embedded inside the browser-profile list payload.

name.description: Human-readable alias for the proxy. Optional. Used in the UI to fastly recognize or search for the proxy. If skipped, the proxy will be named after it is host, port, login (if provided) and password (if provided), i.e. `protocol://login:password@host:port`.

changeIpUrl.description: Indicates whether a change-IP endpoint is configured for this proxy.

provider.description: Provider identifier if the proxy originates from a supported provider. Optional.

oneOf.description: Result of the last connectivity check. The check is performed by the app on your PCs.

createdAt.description: Date and time when the last connectivity check was performed.

status.description: Indicates whether the proxy passed the connectivity check (`true`) or failed (`false`).

ip.description: Public IP address detected during the connectivity check.

country.description: Country associated with the detected IP address.

region.description: Region (state or province) associated with the detected IP address.

city.description: City associated with the detected IP address.

timezone.description: Time zone of the detected IP address location.

found.description: Number of blacklist services that reported the proxy's IP.

total.description: Total number of blacklist services that were checked.

blacklists.description: Names of blacklist services where the proxy's IP was found.

browser_profile_list_item.description: A complete browser profile object. A browser profile emulates a separate, fully-isolated browser instance with its own fingerprint, proxy, cookies, and other persistent data. Most write-operations that reference a browser profile will accept a subset of the fields below – this schema intentionally contains **all** attributes that can be returned by the "List browser profiles" and "Get browser profile" endpoints so that generated clients have full type-safety.

teamId.description: Team identifier that owns the profile.

userId.description: Identifier of the user that initially created (owns) the profile.

name.description: Human-readable profile name shown in the UI.

tags.description: Free-form tags attached to the profile.

platform.description: Host OS that the profile mimics.

browserType.description: Browser engine used by the profile (always `anty`, legacy field).

mainWebsite.description: Website that is indicated as the main website of the profile in the UI. Used for the profile icon, some spoof tweaks, etc.

proxy.description: Proxy used by the profile (if any).

access.description: CRUD permissions of the current user with respect to this profile.

pinned.description: Indicates whether the profile is pinned in the UI for the current user.

folder.description: Identifier of the folder the profile belongs to (if any).

homepages.description: Custom startup pages configured for the profile.

fontsMode.description: Strategy used for fonts override – `auto` (default) or `manual`.

macAddress.description: MAC-address settings. Newer payloads return an object with `mode`/`value`; legacy profiles may return a plain string or string array.

deviceName.description: Device name settings object (new format) or legacy string/array.

isHiddenProfileName.description: If `true`, the profile name is hidden in WebGL fingerprint (anti-detection measure).

disableLoadWebCameraAndCookies.description: If `true`, the built-in browser will not load a web-camera and will disable cookies.

enableArgIsChromeIcon.description: When `true` an additional launch argument is injected so the profile icon mimics Chrome.

totalSessionDuration.description: Total time (in seconds) the profile has been running since creation.

lastStartTime.description: Date & time when the profile was last started.

updated_at.description: Timestamp is always returned in Europe/Moscow (UTC+03:00) timezone – the backend converts it from UTC.

value.description: Full UA string to apply when `mode` is `manual`.

ipAddress.description: Public IP to expose via WebRTC when `mode` is `manual`.

value.description: Base-64 encoded canvas image hash when `mode` is `manual`.

noise.description: Array of floats (0-1) used as offsets when `mode` is `noise`.

value.description: Pre-calculated WebGL hash when `mode` is `manual`.

webgl2Maximum.description: Mapping of WebGL2 `MAX_*` parameter names to their reported values. Each value is either an integer or a two-element integer array (for parameters like `MAX_VIEWPORT_DIMS`).

noise.description: Array of floats applied as offsets when `mode` is `noise`.

value.description: IANA time-zone identifier when `mode` is `manual`, e.g. `Europe/Paris`.
Preferred locale (e.g. `en-US`).
Number of logical CPU cores to report.
Approximate device RAM (in GiB).

storagePath.description: Internal path for browser profile data.

transferStatus.description: When the profile is being transferred to another team this field shows the transfer status.

transferToEmail.description: Email address of the receiver when the profile transfer is in progress.

transferHandleDate.description: Date & time when the transfer request was handled (accepted or cancelled).

transferWithProxy.description: Indicates whether the proxy was moved together with the profile during transfer.

requirePassword.description: Indicates whether the profile is protected by a password (any modifying request must provide the password).

recoverCount.description: Number of recovery snapshots available for the profile.

cloudSyncDisabled.description: When true, cloud sync is disabled for the profile on the current team account.

cloudSyncDisabledOnMachineId.description: Identifier of the machine that disabled cloud sync (if any).

notes.description: User-defined notes. Recent payloads return the full rich-text object; older responses may contain plain string or be null.

name.description: Human-readable alias for the proxy. Optional. Used in the UI to fastly recognize or search for the proxy. If skipped, the proxy will be named after it is host, port, login (if provided) and password (if provided), i.e. `protocol://login:password@host:port`.

changeIpUrl.description: URL that triggers IP rotation for the proxy. Optional. Some mobile proxy provider give the ability to rotate IP by sending a request to a special URL, and our app gives user a button to do that.

provider.description: Provider identifier if the proxy originates from a supported provider. Optional.

ip.description: Public IP address detected for this proxy (same value as `lastCheck.ip` in legacy payloads). Returned by the backend.

savedByUser.description: Indicates whether the proxy was explicitly created by the user (`true`) or auto-imported (`false`). Returned by the backend.

browser_profiles_count.description: Number of browser profiles linked to this proxy (that are using this proxy).

oneOf.description: Result of the last connectivity check. The check is performed by the app on your PCs.

createdAt.description: Date and time when the last connectivity check was performed.

status.description: Indicates whether the proxy passed the connectivity check (`true`) or failed (`false`).

ip.description: Public IP address detected during the connectivity check.

country.description: Country associated with the detected IP address.

region.description: Region (state or province) associated with the detected IP address.

city.description: City associated with the detected IP address.

timezone.description: Time zone of the detected IP address location.

found.description: Number of blacklist services that reported the proxy's IP.

total.description: Total number of blacklist services that were checked.

blacklists.description: Names of blacklist services where the proxy's IP was found.

compress.description:
Compress determines if the rotated log files should be compressed
using gzip. The default is not to perform compression.

localTime.description:
LocalTime determines if the time used for formatting the timestamps in
backup files is the computer's local time. The default is to use UTC
time.

maxAge.description:
MaxAge is the maximum number of days to retain old log files based on the
timestamp encoded in their filename.  Note that a day is defined as 24
hours and may not exactly correspond to calendar days due to daylight
savings, leap seconds, etc. The default is not to remove old log files
based on age.

maxBackups.description:
MaxBackups is the maximum number of old log files to retain.  The default
is to retain all old log files (though MaxAge may still cause them to get
deleted.)

maxSize.description:
MaxSize is the maximum size in megabytes of the log file before it gets
rotated. It defaults to 100 megabytes.

name.description: Human-readable profile name shown in the UI.

tags.description: Free-form tags attached to the profile.

tabs.description: Array of URLs to open on the first launch of the profile.

platform.description: Host OS that the profile mimics.

platformVersion.description: Host OS version. Fetch from the fingerprint API.

browserType.description: Browser engine used by the profile (always `anty`, legacy field).

mainWebsite.description: Main website associated with the profile. Affects profile icon and certain spoofing tweaks.

statusId.description: Identifier of the status to set for the profile. You can automatically set the status of a profile by attaching the status id to the profile.

proxy.description: Proxy used by the profile (if needed).

args.description: Custom launch arguments used by the internal browser.

notes.description: Arbitrary notes attached to the profile; each note is a free-form object.

login.description: Login for proxy authentication. Required if `password` is supplied.

password.description: Password for proxy authentication. Required if `login` is supplied.

fingerprint.description: Raw fingerprint block returned by the fingerprint API. When present the backend derives flattened fields automatically.

uaFullVersion.description: Chrome full version string associated with the fingerprint.

folderId.description: Identifier of the folder the profile belongs to (if any).

homepages.description: Custom startup pages configured for the profile.

newHomepages.description: Homepages to create and attach in one call (advanced). Each item mirrors the Homepage entity fields.

fontsMode.description: Strategy used for fonts override – `auto` (default) or `manual`.

value.description: MAC-address to apply when `mode` is `manual`.
Device name used for existing profiles (legacy field).

valueNew.description: Device name used for new profiles when `mode` is `manual`.

isHiddenProfileName.description: If `true`, the profile name is hidden in WebGL fingerprint (anti-detection measure).

disableLoadWebCameraAndCookies.description: If `true`, the built-in browser will not load a web-camera and will disable cookies.

enableArgIsChromeIcon.description: When `true` an additional launch argument is injected so the profile icon mimics Chrome.

doNotTrack.description: Report `navigator.doNotTrack = 1`. Take it from the fingerprint object if you do not understand what to put here.

value.description: Full UA string to apply when `mode` is `manual`.

ipAddress.description: Public IP to expose via WebRTC when `mode` is `manual`.

value.description: Base-64 encoded canvas image hash when `mode` is `manual`.

noise.description: Array of floats (0-1) used as offsets when `mode` is `noise`.

value.description: Pre-calculated WebGL hash when `mode` is `manual`.
WebGPU fingerprint hash when `mode` is `manual`.

webgl2Maximum.description: Mapping of WebGL2 `MAX_*` parameter names to their reported values. Each value is either an integer or a two-element integer array (for parameters like `MAX_VIEWPORT_DIMS`).

noise.description: Array of floats applied as offsets when `mode` is `noise`.

value.description: IANA time-zone identifier when `mode` is `manual`, e.g. `Europe/Paris`.
Preferred locale (e.g. `en-US`).
Number of logical CPU cores to report.
Approximate device RAM (in GiB).

connectionSaveData.description: Indicates whether the connection uses data-saver mode (0 or 1) (flattened field).

platformName.description: Full platform name reported by the browser fingerprint.

cpuArchitecture.description: CPU architecture string reported by the browser fingerprint.

osVersion.description: Operating-system version string derived from the fingerprint.

screenWidth.description: Reported screen width in pixels (flattened from the screen block).

screenHeight.description: Reported screen height in pixels (flattened from the screen block).

vendorSub.description: Value of navigator.vendorSub reported by the profile.

productSub.description: Value of navigator.productSub reported by the profile.

vendor.description: Value of navigator.vendor reported by the profile.

product.description: Value of navigator.product reported by the profile.

appCodeName.description: Value of navigator.appCodeName reported by the profile.

mediaDevices.description: List of media devices returned by navigator.mediaDevices.enumerateDevices().

userFields.description: Arbitrary custom fields supplied by the user; each item is an object with user-defined keys.

ports.description: Port protection settings for the profile.

mode.description: Protection mode. Use `protect` to block a set of ports.

ids.description: Identifiers of Browser profiles to delete.

password.description: Profile password. Used only when the request deletes **exactly one** profile and that profile is protected by a password. Must be omitted in all other cases.

forceDelete.description: When **true** profiles are permanently removed instead of being moved to the "Basket". Free-plan workspaces must supply this flag to confirm the intent since the basket is not available on free plans.

items.description: Array of Browser profile definitions to create. Each element follows the same schema as a single-profile creation payload (see `BrowserProfileCreateInput`).

common.description:
Optional object with properties that are applied to **every** element of `items` before validation. Keys and value types mirror `BrowserProfileCreateInput`. If a property exists in both `common` and an individual item, the item's value overrides the one from `common`.
Note: For bulk creation the backend only considers top-level fields of each item. Do not provide a nested `fingerprint` object here or inside `items` – it will be ignored. Instead, place the fingerprint-derived fields at the root level (e.g. `useragent`, `uaFullVersion`, `cpu`, `memory`, `screenWidth`, `screenHeight`, `connectionDownlink`, `connectionRtt`, `connectionEffectiveType`, `connectionSaveData`, `webglInfo`, `webgl2Maximum`, etc.).

ids.description: Identifiers of Browser profiles to transfer. The caller **must** be the owner of these profiles or have the `share` permission granted for them; otherwise the request will be rejected with a 403 error.

username.description: E-mail address of the Dolphin{anty} user who will **receive** the transferred profiles. The user may belong to the same team or to a different team/workspace. If the user is not found the request succeeds silently but no transfer is performed (this matches the backend behaviour).

withProxy.description:
When `true` and a profile has a proxy attached, that proxy object **and its credentials** are moved to the receiver together with the profile. If omitted or set to `false` the profile is transferred **without** a proxy (its `proxyId` is reset to `0`).
Only proxies **owned by the sender team** can be transferred; shared or external proxies cannot be moved and will be stripped from the profile irrespective of this flag.

browser_profile.description: A complete browser profile object. A browser profile emulates a separate, fully-isolated browser instance with its own fingerprint, proxy, cookies, and other persistent data. Most write-operations that reference a browser profile will accept a subset of the fields below – this schema intentionally contains **all** attributes that can be returned by the "List browser profiles" and "Get browser profile" endpoints so that generated clients have full type-safety.

teamId.description: Team identifier that owns the profile.

userId.description: Identifier of the user that initially created (owns) the profile.

name.description: Human-readable profile name shown in the UI.

tags.description: Free-form tags attached to the profile.

platform.description: Host OS that the profile mimics.

browserType.description: Browser engine used by the profile (always `anty`, legacy field).

mainWebsite.description: Website that is indicated as the main website of the profile in the UI. Used for the profile icon, some spoof tweaks, etc.

proxy.description: Proxy used by the profile (if any).

access.description: CRUD permissions of the current user with respect to this profile.

pinned.description: Indicates whether the profile is pinned in the UI for the current user.

folder.description: Identifier of the folder the profile belongs to (if any).

homepages.description: Custom startup pages configured for the profile.

fontsMode.description: Strategy used for fonts override – `auto` (default) or `manual`.

macAddress.description: MAC-address used inside the profile (string or string array, depending on mode).

deviceName.description: Randomised device name reported by the browser profile.

isHiddenProfileName.description: If `true`, the profile name is hidden in WebGL fingerprint (anti-detection measure).

disableLoadWebCameraAndCookies.description: If `true`, the built-in browser will not load a web-camera and will disable cookies.

enableArgIsChromeIcon.description: When `true` an additional launch argument is injected so the profile icon mimics Chrome.

totalSessionDuration.description: Total time (in seconds) the profile has been running since creation.

lastStartTime.description: Date & time when the profile was last started.

lastRunUuid.description: Unique run identifier reported by the client application.

running.description: Indicates whether the profile is currently running on any user machine.

value.description: Full UA string to apply when `mode` is `manual`.

ipAddress.description: Public IP to expose via WebRTC when `mode` is `manual`.

value.description: Base-64 encoded canvas image hash when `mode` is `manual`.

noise.description: Array of floats (0-1) used as offsets when `mode` is `noise`.

value.description: Pre-calculated WebGL hash when `mode` is `manual`.

webgl2Maximum.description: Mapping of WebGL2 `MAX_*` parameter names to their reported values. Each value is either an integer or a two-element integer array (for parameters like `MAX_VIEWPORT_DIMS`).

noise.description: Array of floats applied as offsets when `mode` is `noise`.

value.description: IANA time-zone identifier when `mode` is `manual`, e.g. `Europe/Paris`.
Preferred locale (e.g. `en-US`).
Number of logical CPU cores to report.
Approximate device RAM (in GiB).

storagePath.description: Internal path for browser profile data.

transferStatus.description: When the profile is being transferred to another team this field shows the transfer status.

transferToEmail.description: Email address of the receiver when the profile transfer is in progress.

transferHandleDate.description: Date & time when the transfer request was handled (accepted or cancelled).

transferWithProxy.description: Indicates whether the proxy was moved together with the profile during transfer.

requirePassword.description: Indicates whether the profile is protected by a password (any modifying request must provide the password).

notes.description: User-defined notes attached to the profile. Only present when the profile has non-empty notes.

allOf.description:
A complete browser-profile object as returned by the "Get browser profile" endpoint.
Contains every attribute currently emitted by the backend. It **extends** the
generic `BrowserProfile` schema with additional fields such as UA version,
WebGPU, advanced device information etc.

args.description: Launch arguments that the desktop client will pass to the browser engine.

tabs.description: Opened tabs information returned only by the Get-profile endpoint.

bookmarks.description: Team bookmarks applicable to this profile.

extensions.description: Browser extensions associated with this profile.

password.description: Profile password. Required only when the targeted Browser profile is protected by a password. If the profile is not password-protected this field MUST be omitted.

forceDelete.description: When **true** the profile is permanently removed instead of being moved to the "Basket". Free-plan workspaces must supply this flag to confirm the intent since the basket is not available on free plans.

browser_profile_update.description:
Input payload for updating an existing Browser profile.
This schema mirrors every field accepted by `BrowserProfileCreateInput` but **all attributes are optional** –
you can supply only the properties you want to modify. Any omitted properties remain unchanged.

For a detailed description of every field refer to `BrowserProfileCreateInput` – the set of properties is
identical.

browser_profile_access_multi_edit.description:
Input payload for **Share access to multiple browser profiles** endpoint.
Grants or revokes specific CRUD permissions for one or more users across
the selected Browser profiles in bulk.

ids.description: List of Browser profile identifiers that should be affected.

users.description:
List of user-permission descriptors. Every object configures permissions
for a single user. When `action` is `add`, the specified permission
flags are **granted** (set to `true`). When `action` is `remove`, the
same flags are **revoked** (set to `false`).

id.description: Unique identifier of the user who will receive/lose access.

view.description: Permission to read the Browser profile.

update.description: Permission to modify the Browser profile.

delete.description: Permission to delete the Browser profile.

share.description: Permission to share the Browser profile further with other users.

usage.description: Permission to launch the Browser profile (run sessions).

action.description:
Specifies whether the listed permissions should be **added** (`add`) or
**removed** (`remove`). If `remove` is used, *all* supplied permission
flags must be `true` in order to fully revoke the access row.

browser_profile_access_update.description:
Input payload for **Share access to browser profile** endpoint. Updates
CRUD permissions for one or more users with respect to a **single**
Browser profile.

users.description:
List of user-permission descriptors. Same shape as in the bulk endpoint
but uses the `userId` key as required identifier of the target user.

userId.description: Unique identifier of the user whose permissions you modify.

view.description: Permission to read the Browser profile.

update.description: Permission to modify the Browser profile.

delete.description: Permission to delete the Browser profile.

share.description: Permission to share the Browser profile further with other users.

usage.description: Permission to launch the Browser profile (run sessions).

password.description:
Profile password (required only when the profile is protected with a
password). Must match the previously set password to allow permission
changes.  
Ignored when profile is not protected.

fingerprint.description:
A full browser fingerprint snapshot. This structure is returned by the
**“Get fingerprint”** endpoint and is intended to be used as-is when
creating browser profiles.

Most scalar properties are copied verbatim from real-world devices in our
internal dataset – no additional processing is applied. Keep the payload as
is to achieve maximum anti-detect quality.

availWidth.description: Available screen width in CSS pixels.

availHeight.description: Available screen height in CSS pixels.

pixelDepth.description: Pixel depth reported by the browser.

connection.description: Network connection metrics from the *Network Information API*.

downlink.description: Estimated effective bandwidth in megabits per second.

saveData.description: Whether *Data Saver* mode is enabled on the device.

platform.description: Host operating system reported by the browser.

userAgent.example: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/113.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

architecture.description: CPU architecture string reported by Chrome.

name.description: Human-readable operating system name.
Browser engine name.

voices.description: JSON-encoded list of `SpeechSynthesisVoice` objects supported by the device.

fonts.description: JSON-encoded list of fonts installed on the system. Present only when the account has *Fonts override* permission.

browserType.description: Browser family for which the fingerprint was generated.

uaFullVersion.description: Full UA version retrieved from the *User-Agent Client Hints* API.

webgl2Maximum.description: Mapping of WebGL2 `MAX_*` constants to their reported values. Each value is either an integer or a two-element integer array.

webgpu.description: Serialized GPU adapter info captured from the *WebGPU* API.

webgl2Maximum.description: Mapping of WebGL2 `MAX_*` constants to their reported values. Each value is either an integer or a two-element integer array.

font.description:
A single font record coming from the Dolphin{anty} fingerprint dataset.  
Each entry represents a **font family** that realistically exists on the
specified platform and can therefore be safely used when overriding the
`fonts` fingerprint block.

id.description: Unique identifier of this font record inside the dataset.

font.description: The full font family name (e.g. the value returned by `Canvas` API or available in CSS `font-family`).

type.description:
Category describing how common the font is on the target platform.  

- **`default`** – ships with the operating system and is almost always present.  
- **`extra`** – frequently observed additional fonts (may be missing on a clean install).  

Treat the value as an **opaque string** – new categories may appear in the future.

os.description: Operating system this font belongs to.

homepage.description:
Homepage record used as a custom start page in Dolphin{anty} browser profiles. Each
homepage points to a specific URL and can optionally be shared with the whole team.

teamId.description: Identifier of the team that owns the homepage.

userId.description: Identifier of the user who created the homepage.

name.description: Human-readable name shown in the UI (not the URL itself).

url.description: Absolute URL the browser opens on startup, including scheme (`https://`).

sharedToEntireTeam.description:
When `true` the homepage becomes visible and attachable by every member of the team.
When `false` the homepage is private to the creator.

mainWebsite.description:
One or more "main website" categories indicating where the homepage is primarily used.
This data powers contextual suggestions in the UI.

homepage_create.description: Parameters required to create a homepage.

name.description: Human-readable name shown in the UI.

url.description: Destination URL to open on browser start.

sharedToEntireTeam.description: Whether to share the homepage with the entire team (`true`) or keep it private (`false`).

mainWebsite.description: Website category/categories relevant to this homepage.

allOf.description: Fields identical to creation but intended for partial updates – supply only the attributes you want to change.

bookmark.description: Bookmark record used as a custom browser bookmark (quick-access link) inside Dolphin{anty}. A bookmark always points to a particular web page and, when shared, becomes available to every member of the team.

teamId.description: Identifier of the team that owns the bookmark.

userId.description: Identifier of the user who created the bookmark.

name.description: Human-readable label shown in the Dolphin{anty} UI.

url.description: Absolute URL the built-in browser navigates to when the bookmark is clicked.

favicon.description: Link to the page favicon. The backend attempts to auto-fetch the icon on creation, therefore this field is usually **null** in the request payload and populated only in the response.

sharedToEntireTeam.description: When `true` the bookmark is visible and attachable by **all** team members. When `false` it stays private to the creator.

mainWebsite.description:
One or more "main website" categories designating the environment where the bookmark is predominantly used. Accepted values are the same as for browser profiles and homepages.

Used for automatically applying the bookmark to the browser profiles with the same main website.

bookmark_create.description: Parameters required to create a bookmark.

name.description: Human-readable label shown in the UI.

url.description: Destination URL to open when the bookmark is activated.

sharedToEntireTeam.description: Whether to share the bookmark with the entire team (`true`) or keep it private (`false`).

mainWebsite.description: Website category/categories relevant to this bookmark.

allOf.description: Fields identical to creation but intended for partial updates – supply only the attributes you want to change.

team_user.description:
A team user represents a member of the current team account.
Team members share access to browser profiles, folders and other resources
according to their role and access settings described below.

id.description: Unique identifier of the user inside Dolphin{anty}.

teamId.description: Identifier of the team the user belongs to.

username.description: E-mail used to log into Dolphin{anty}.

displayName.description: Human-readable name shown in the UI. When null the username is used instead.

telegram.description: Telegram username linked to the account (without the `@` sign).

role.description:
Role of the user inside the team.
• `admin` – full control over the team account
• `teamlead` – manages a subset of users and resources
• `user` – regular member with limited permissions

teamleads.description: List of identifiers of team-lead users supervising the member.

access_setting.description: Fine-grained limits applied to the user.

canCreateBp.description: When `true` the user is allowed to create new browser profiles.

unlimitedBp.description: When `true` the user is not limited by the `bpLimit` quota.

bpLimit.description: Maximum number of browser profiles the user can create (ignored when `unlimitedBp` is true).

bpCount.description: Current amount of browser profiles already created by the user.

created_at.description: Date & time when the user was created (Europe/Moscow, UTC+03:00).

updated_at.description: Date & time of the last update (Europe/Moscow, UTC+03:00).

team_user_create.description:
Payload for creating a new member inside the current team account.
Only users with the `admin` role can add team members.

username.description: Unique e-mail address used as the login for the new user.

password.description:
Login password for the new user.
Must be 8–512 characters long.

role.description: Role to assign to the new user.

displayName.description: Human-readable name shown in the UI.

teamleads.description:
Identifiers of team-lead users supervising the member.
Can only be provided when `role` is `user`.

canCreateBp.description:
Grants the permission to create new browser profiles.
When omitted the permission defaults to the backend logic depending on the requester role.

unlimitedBp.description:
When `true` the user is not limited by `bpLimit`.
Must be provided **together** with `canCreateBp=true`.

bpLimit.description:
Hard limit for the amount of browser profiles the user can own.
Ignored when `unlimitedBp` is set to `true`.

team_user_update.description:
Set of fields that can be modified for an existing team user. 
All properties are optional – provide only those you need to change.

password.description: New login password. When omitted the password is not changed.

username.description: New login e-mail. Must be unique within Dolphin{anty}.

displayName.description: New human-readable name shown in the UI.

role.description: New role to assign to the user.

teamleads.description: Identifiers of team-lead users supervising the member. Passing an empty array removes supervisors.

canCreateBp.description: Grant or revoke the permission to create browser profiles.

unlimitedBp.description:
When `true` the user is not limited by `bpLimit`.
Must be provided **together** with `canCreateBp=true`.

bpLimit.description:
Hard limit for the amount of browser profiles the user can own.
Ignored when `unlimitedBp` is set to `true`.

local_storage_item.description:
Single Local Storage snapshot for one origin. When exporting Local Storage the backend
groups key/value pairs by their associated **domain** (origin). Each object therefore
contains data for exactly one domain.

domain.description:
Origin (domain) the Local Storage belongs to. A special placeholder value
`dolphin-anty-export` is used by the backend when the export represents a
flattened dump that mixes keys from multiple origins.

data.description:
Dictionary of Local Storage entries as they are kept inside Chromium’s
LevelDB. **Both keys and values are Base-64 encoded strings**. Consumers
normally treat this object as an opaque blob – you rarely need to decode
it manually.

local_storage_export.description:
Complete Local Storage dump produced by the export endpoints. The array
contains one or more `LocalStorageItem` objects – one per origin.

profileId.description: Identifier of the browser profile that should receive the Local Storage.

transfer.description:
Indicates whether the operation is executed while the profile is being transferred
between teams. When `1`/`true` the backend uses an alternative storage location.

browserProfilePassword.description:
Password protecting the target profile (if `requirePassword` is enabled).
Omit or pass `null` when not used.

cloudSyncDisabled.description:
When `true` skips downloading the existing datadir from cloud storage and does not
push the archive back after import.

cookie_item.description:
Single browser cookie entry as stored in Chromium’s SQLite `Cookies` DB. Each
object represents **one** cookie (name/value pair).

domain.description: Hostname the cookie applies to (leading dot allowed for subdomains).

name.description: Cookie name.

value.description: Raw cookie value.

expirationDate.description: Expiration timestamp in **milliseconds** since Unix epoch (Chromium format).

secure.description: When `true`, the cookie is only sent over HTTPS.

httpOnly.description: When `true`, the cookie is inaccessible to JavaScript (`document.cookie`).

cookies_export.description:
Flat list of cookies produced by the export endpoints. The array contains
zero or more `CookieItem` objects.

profileId.description: Identifier of the browser profile that will receive the cookies.

transfer.description:
Indicates whether the operation is executed while the profile is being
transferred between teams. When `1`/`true` the backend uses an alternative
storage location.

browserProfilePassword.description:
Password protecting the target profile (if `requirePassword` is enabled).
Omit or pass `null` when not used.

cloudSyncDisabled.description:
When `true` skips downloading the existing datadir from cloud storage and
does not push the archive back after import.

browserProfiles.description:
Array describing the profiles to export cookies from. Each element must
contain at least the profile `id` and `name`.

name.description: Human-readable profile name (used only for file naming).

transfer.description: Same meaning as in the import endpoint. Optional – defaults to `0`.

browserProfilePassword.description: Password protecting the profile when `requirePassword` is enabled.

doNotSave.description:
When `true`, the backend does **not** write `.txt` files to the host’s
*Downloads* folder. The cookie data is still returned in the response.

cookie_robot_start.description:
Payload accepted by `POST /cookies/{browserProfileId}/robot`.
Instructs Dolphin{anty} to visit every supplied URL in an invisible browser
page and persist the cookies produced by the websites into the specified
browser profile.

data.description:
One or more absolute URLs (including the `http`/`https` scheme) that the
robot must open **sequentially**. After each page finishes loading (the
network is idle) the backend waits a couple of seconds for late async
requests, then extracts all cookies, closes the tab and proceeds to the
next URL.

headless.description:
When `true` the Anty browser is launched in **headless** mode (no GUI). In
this mode the robot consumes less resources and works on machines without
a window manager. Internally this flag also disables opening profile tabs
to speed up startup.

imageless.description:
When `true` the Chromium network stack skips downloading images. This
significantly speeds up crawling heavy pages when only cookies are
required.

plan.description:
*(Optional)* Reserved debugging field. The value is ignored and replaced
by the authenticated account’s actual plan on the backend. Pass `null` or
omit entirely.

browserProfilePassword.description:
Password protecting the target profile (needed when the profile was
created with `requirePassword = true`). Provide the plaintext password so
the robot can decrypt and write the *Cookies* SQLite database. Omit or
pass `null` when the profile is not locked.

browser_profile_start.description:
Payload accepted by `POST /browser_profiles/{browserProfileId}/start`.
Controls how the local Anty browser instance is launched. All fields are
optional – when omitted the backend falls back to the defaults used by the
Desktop application.

screenWidth.description: Logical width of the your real screen. Used as a fallback for the screen width if you use "screen" spoof in `real` mode.

screenHeight.description: Logical height of the your real screen. Used as a fallback for the screen height if you use "screen" spoof in `real` mode.

dpr.description:
Device-pixel ratio of your real screen. On Windows the backend internally
multiplies `screenWidth`/`screenHeight` by this factor to emulate HiDPI
(retina) displays. On macOS this will likely be 2, and on Windows it will depend on the display settings. Used only if you use "screen" spoof in `real` mode.

headless.description:
When `true` the browser is launched in Chromium **headless** mode (no
GUI). This flag implicitly sets `noTabs = true`.

automation.description:
Enables remote automation APIs (Puppeteer / Playwright / Selenium). When set to
`true` the successful response contains the `automation` object with the
connection details (`port`, `wsEndpoint`).

This feature is **unavailable on the Free plan** – the backend returns
**HTTP 402** when requested.

noTabs.description:
Skips restoring the profile tabs after launch and leaves the instance
completely blank. Automatically forced to `true` when both `headless`
and `automation` are enabled.

is_owned.description: Marks the current machine as the **owner** of the running Synchronizer session – this makes other browsers copy any actions you do in this browser.

name_session.description: Any identifier, e.g. UUIDv4, used to identify the running synchronizer session. All profiles in the same session will mirror the actions of **Owned** profile.

transfer.description: *(Internal)* Starts the browser in **transfer** mode, which means the profile is currently is in transfer between teams. Normal clients should omit the field.

browserProfilePassword.description:
Plain-text password protecting the target profile. Required only when the
profile was created with specific password set.

login_with_token.description:
Payload accepted by `POST /login-with-token`. Stores a
remote Dolphin{anty} **JWT token** inside the Local API and optionally applies
pre-fetched feature flags for the current machine.

token.description:
The **exact** JWT bearer token generated in the web panel
(https://dolphin-anty.com/panel). The value is stored locally (encrypted
if the OS keychain is available) and will be attached to all subsequent
requests sent by the Local API to the remote Dolphin{anty} endpoints.
